assertions.evidence: A 100% pass rate is a measurement problem, not a finish line... not 'the skill is done.'
Mine real failures, not imagined ones... Each rejected output becomes a case... Add adversarial cases that target the skill's own rules.
Observed trigger rate and false-positive trigger rate... Rewrite quality beyond binary assertions... Judge drift.
No, it should fail, and the fact that it would pass is the bug.
It rewards token presence, which the model can satisfy without expressing the underlying mechanism... It is slop wearing the right vocabulary.
The rewrite states what is saved and what is re-run... a failed step retries from its last checkpoint while completed steps keep their outputs.
Stop treating the phrase list as the spec. It is one detector among several... a model that simply stops emitting those strings will pass.
Unsupported specificity... Fake precision / false provenance... an authority is invoked without a nameable source.
Add a failure-corpus entry for unsupported specificity and false provenance... Add evals that test provenance directly.
This is false-positive trigger drift. The skill is firing on inputs it should ignore.
Add near-miss negatives to the trigger evals... 'fix the typo in this sentence'... Each should assert that the skill does NOT fire.
Tighten the description at the boundary, not at the core... Avoid the blunt fix of deleting broad writing triggers.
Do not break the tie on taste... it is not actually a disagreement about preference; it is a disagreement that the current process has no evidence standard to resolve.
Require each grader to quote the specific before/after span they are judging... Every score carries a quote from the rewrite or the source.
A recurring disagreement is a signal the rubric is ambiguous... Tighten the criterion... and add this case to the suite as a worked example.
That is well within the noise you expect from seed variation and judge nondeterminism, so it is not evidence the change worked.
Run a paired significance test on those deltas... a sign-flip permutation test or a paired bootstrap over per-case score differences. A raw count of passes is not a test.
The decision to merge belongs to the held-out split, which was not used during iteration... let that gate the merge.
This is LLM-as-judge self-preference. When the same model family drafts the candidate and grades it, the judge systematically favors text that matches its own family's style.
Add at least one judge from a different model family and require agreement... Add human spot-checks on the contested cases.
What does not fix it: rewording the judge prompt... on their own they are not sufficient.
